papers.data_used_hu: EFIGE (European Firms in a Global Economy) keresztország-felmérés ~15 000, legalább 10 főt foglalkoztató feldolgozóipari vállalatról hét országban — Ausztria, Franciaország, Németország, Magyarország, Olaszország, Spanyolország, Egyesült Királyság — 2010-es kérdezéssel és 2001–2009-es mérlegadatokkal.
papers.policy_relevance: Direct message for Hungarian Ministry of Economy: standalone export-promotion schemes (e.g., HEPA-type support) give weaker returns than programmes that combine export support with R&D and innovation subsidies. Concrete policy option is an integrated "one roof" support unit bundling HIPA/HEPA tools with innovation grants. Based on pre-2010 data; the rise of GVCs and nearshoring since may have shifted the balance further towards innovation.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlen üzenet a magyar Gazdasági Minisztériumnak: az önálló exportösztönző programok (pl. HEPA-típusú támogatások) gyengébb hozamot adnak, mint azok a programok, amelyek exporttámogatást kapcsolnak össze K+F-fel és innovációs támogatással. Konkrét javaslat egy integrált „egy tető alatti" támogatási egység, amely összekapcsolja a HIPA/HEPA eszközöket az innovációs támogatásokkal. A 2010 előtti adatok alapján készült; a globális értékláncok átrendeződése és a nearshoring azóta tovább tolhatta a mérleget az innováció felé.

popular_links.title: Internationalisation and innovation of firms: Give them one roof

papers.title: Coalitional Rationalizability*
papers.abstract: This paper investigates how groups or coalitions of players can act in their collective interest in non-cooperative normal form games even if equilibrium play is not assumed. The main idea is that each member of a coalition will confine play to a subset of their strategies if it is in their mutual interest to do so. An iterative procedure of restrictions is used to define a non-cooperative solution concept, the set of coalitionally rationalizable strategies. The procedure is analogous to iterative deletion of never best response strategies, but operates on implicit agreements by different coalitions. The solution set is a nonempty subset of the rationalizable strategies. I thank Dilip Abreu for support and extremely valuable comments at all stages of

papers.title: Theories of coalitional rationality
Asymmetric Networks in Two-Sided Markets
papers.abstract: This paper investigates pricing decisions and network choices in two-sided markets with network externalities. Consumers are heterogeneous in how much they value the externality. Imposing restrictions on the extent of coordination failure among consumers generates clear qualitative conclusions about equilibrium market configurations. Multiple asymmetric networks can coexist in equilibrium, both in the case of a monopolist network provider and in the case of competing providers. These equilibria have the property that one network is cheaper and larger on one side, while the other network is cheaper and larger on the other side. Product differentiation is endogenized by consumers' network choices. (JEL D85, L12, L13, L14, D42, D43)
papers.summary_en: Markets with network externalities — credit-card platforms, marketplaces, app stores — are often modelled as having a single dominant network. This theory paper shows that asymmetric equilibria, where two networks coexist with one cheaper/larger on each side of the market, are natural when consumers differ in how much they value the externality. One network may be cheaper and larger on the consumer side while the other is cheaper and larger on the merchant side. Product differentiation arises endogenously from consumer sorting.
papers.summary_hu: A hálózati externáliával rendelkező piacokat — bankkártya-platformok, piacterek, app-áruházak — gyakran egyetlen domináns hálózattal rendelkező piacokként modellezik. A tanulmány megmutatja, hogy aszimmetrikus egyensúlyok, amelyekben két hálózat együtt létezik és mindegyik a piac egyik oldalán olcsóbb/nagyobb, természetesek, amikor a fogyasztók eltérően értékelik az externáliát. Az egyik hálózat a fogyasztói oldalon lehet olcsóbb és nagyobb, míg a másik a kereskedői oldalon. A termékdifferenciálás a fogyasztói szortírozás révén endogén módon alakul ki.
papers.data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti tanulmány; nincs empirikus adat.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian competition policy (GVH) in two-sided markets — card payments, online marketplaces, food-delivery platforms. Suggests that the existence of multiple platforms does not necessarily imply they are poor substitutes; they may be deliberately differentiated. Merger analysis and dominance assessments should allow for asymmetric-but-coexisting equilibria rather than assuming winner-takes-all dynamics. Setting is abstract; applying to concrete cases requires empirical measurement of consumer heterogeneity.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Releváns a magyar versenypolitikához (GVH) a kétoldalú piacokon — kártyás fizetések, online piacterek, ételkiszállító platformok. A tanulmány arra utal, hogy a több platform létezése nem feltétlenül jelenti, hogy rossz helyettesítői egymásnak; lehet, hogy szándékosan differenciáltak. A fúzió-elemzésnek és a dominancia-értékelésnek engedélyeznie kell az aszimmetrikus-de-együttlétező egyensúlyokat, nem pedig feltételeznie a „a győztes mindent visz" dinamikát. A környezet absztrakt; konkrét esetekre alkalmazáshoz a fogyasztói heterogenitás empirikus mérése szükséges.

papers.title: Legislative committees as information intermediaries: A unified theory of committee selection and amendment rules
Hierarchical cheap talk
papers.abstract: We investigate situations in which agents can communicate to each other only through a chain of intermediators, for example because they have to obey institutionalized communication protocols. We assume that all involved in the communication are strategic, and might want to influence the action taken by the final receiver. The set of pure strategy equilibrium outcomes is simple to characterize, monotonic in each intermediator's bias, does not depend on the order of intermediators, and intermediation in these equilibria cannot improve information transmission. However, none of these conclusions hold for mixed equilibria. We provide a partial characterization of mixed equilibria, and offer an economically relevant sufficient condition for every equilibrium to be outcome-equivalent to a pure equilibrium and hence the simple characterization and comparative statics results to hold for the set of all equilibria.

papers.title: A Delegation-Based Theory of Expertise
papers.abstract: We investigate information aggregation and competition in a delegation framework. An uninformed principal is unable to perform a task herself and must choose between one of two biased and imperfectly informed experts. In the focal equilibrium, experts exaggerate their biases, anticipating an ideological winner’s curse. We show that having a second expert can benefit the principal, even when equally or more biased than the first expert. The principal can benefit from commitment to an “element of surprise” and prefers experts with equal rather than opposite biases. (JEL D82, D83)

papers.title: Either or Both Competition: A “Two-Sided” Theory of Advertising with Overlapping Viewerships
papers.abstract: In media markets, consumers spread their attention to several outlets, increasingly so as consumption migrates online. The traditional framework for competition among media outlets rules out this behavior by assumption. We propose a new model that allows consumers to choose multiple outlets and use it to study the effects on advertising levels and the impact of entry and mergers. We identify novel forces which reflect outlets' incentives to control the composition of their customer base. We link consumer preferences and advertising technologies to market outcomes. The model can explain several empirical regularities that are difficult to reconcile with existing models. (JEL D42, D43, L12, L13, L82, L86, M37)

papers.title: Pirates of the Mediterranean: An empirical investigation of bargaining with asymmetric information
papers.abstract: We investigate the effect of delay on prices in bargaining situations using a data set containing thousands of captives ransomed from Barbary pirates between 1575 and 1692. Plausibly exogenous variation in the delay in ransoming provides evidence that negotiating delays decreased the size of ransom payments, and that much of the effect stems from the signalling value of strategic delay, in accordance with theoretical predictions. We also structurally estimate a version of the screening type bargaining model, adjusted to our context, and find that the model fits both the observed prices and acceptance probabilities well.

papers.title: Comment on "Commitment vs. Flexibility"
papers.abstract: This comment corrects two results in the 2006 Econometrica paper by Amador, Werning, and Angeletos (AWA), that features a model in which individuals face a trade-off between flexibility and commitment. First, in contrast to Proposition 1 in AWA, we show that money-burning can be part of the ex ante optimal contract when there are two states. Second, in contrast to Proposition 2 in AWA, we show that money-burning can be imposed at the top (in the highest liquidity shock state), even when there is a continuum of states. We provide corrected versions of the above results. [PUBLICATION ABSTRACT]

papers.title: Delegation and nonmonetary incentives
Investments in social ties, risk sharing, and inequality
papers.abstract: Abstract This article investigates stable and efficient networks in the context of risk sharing, when it is costly to establish and maintain relationships that facilitate risk sharing. We find a novel trade-off between efficiency and equality: the most stable efficient networks also generate the most inequality. We then suppose that individuals can be split into groups, assuming that incomes across groups are less correlated than within a group but relationships across groups are more costly to form. The tension between efficiency and equality extends to these correlated income structures. More-central agents have stronger incentives to form across-group links, reaffirming the efficiency benefits of having highly central agents. Our results are robust to many extensions. In general, endogenously formed networks in the risk-sharing context tend to exhibit highly asymmetric structures, which can lead to stark inequalities in consumption levels.

papers.title: Muslim Family Law, Prenuptial Agreements, and the Emergence of Dowry in Bangladesh<sup>*</sup>
papers.abstract: We explain trends in dowry levels in Bangladesh by drawing attention to an institutional feature of marriage contracts previously ignored in the literature: mehr or traditional Islamic bride-price. We develop a model of marriage contracts in which mehr serves as a barrier to husbands exiting marriage and a component of dowry as an amount that ex ante compensates the groom for the cost of mehr. We investigate how mehr and dowry respond to exogenous changes in the costs of polygamy and divorce, and show that our model gives a different set of predictions than traditional models. We show that major changes in dowry levels took place precisely after the legal changes, corresponding to simultaneous changes in levels of mehr.

papers.title: Loss in the Time of Cholera: Long-Run Impact of a Disease Epidemic on the Urban Landscape
papers.abstract: How do geographically concentrated income shocks influence the long-run spatial distribution of poverty within a city? We examine the impact on housing prices of a cholera epidemic in one neighborhood of nineteenth century London. Ten years after the epidemic, housing prices are significantly lower just inside the catchment area of the water pump that transmitted the disease. Moreover, differences in housing prices persist over the following 160 years. We make sense of these patterns by building a model of a rental market with frictions in which poor tenants exert a negative externality on their neighbors. This showcases how a locally concentrated income shock can persistently change the tenant composition of a block. (JEL D62, O18, R21, R31)
papers.summary_en: A cholera outbreak in 19th-century London was localised to a single contaminated water pump. This paper shows that houses inside the pump's catchment area fell in value after the epidemic and — strikingly — remained cheaper for 160 years. The authors model a rental market with externalities between neighbours: a temporary income shock concentrated in one block can lock that block into a persistent low-income equilibrium. The result is a clean historical demonstration that geographic inequalities in cities can outlast by generations the shocks that caused them.
papers.summary_hu: Egy 19. századi londoni kolera-járvány Soho egyetlen, szennyezett vízszivattyújára lokalizálódott. A tanulmány megmutatja, hogy a szivattyú vízgyűjtő területén lévő házak a járvány után estek értékükben és — meglepő módon — 160 éven át olcsóbbak maradtak. A szerzők egy bérleti piacot modelleznek a szomszédok közötti externáliákkal: egy időleges, egy háztömbre koncentrált jövedelmi sokk tartósan alacsony jövedelmű egyensúlyba tudja zárni a háztömböt. Az eredmény tiszta történelmi bizonyíték arra, hogy a városi térbeli egyenlőtlenségek generációkkal túlélhetik az azokat okozó sokkokat.
papers.data_used: Archival property-price and rental records for the Soho district of London matched to the 1854 cholera outbreak catchment area of the Broad Street pump; price data covers 1844 to the 2010s, combined with census records on neighbourhood composition.
papers.data_used_hu: Archív ingatlanár és bérleti adatok London Soho negyedére az 1854-es kolera-járvány Broad Street-szivattyújának vízgyűjtő területére illesztve; az adatok 1844-től a 2010-es évekig terjednek, népszámlálási adatokkal kiegészítve a környék összetételéről.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian regional and urban policy: neighbourhood-concentrated shocks (factory closures, segregated housing decisions, localised environmental disasters) can create persistent poverty traps that ordinary labour-mobility won't dissolve. Relevant to Ministry of Interior decisions on segregated settlements ("szegregátumok"), targeted urban-renewal funding, and evaluation of place-based interventions. Caveat: 19th-century London had weaker labour mobility than modern Hungary; the mechanism is a plausible channel, not a point prediction.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Magyar regionális és városi politikához: a lokálisan koncentrált sokkok (gyárbezárások, szegregált lakhatási döntések, helyi környezeti katasztrófák) tartós szegénységcsapdákat hozhatnak létre, amelyeket a szokásos munkaerőmobilitás nem old fel. Releváns a Belügyminisztérium szegregátumokkal kapcsolatos döntéseihez, a célzott városrehabilitációs finanszírozáshoz és a helyfüggő beavatkozások értékeléséhez. Figyelmeztetés: a 19. századi Londonnak gyengébb volt a munkaerő-mobilitása, mint a mai Magyarországnak; a mechanizmus plauzibilis csatorna, nem pontos előrejelzés.

papers.title: Informal Risk Sharing with Local Information
papers.abstract: Abstract This article considers the effect of contracting limitations in risk-sharing networks, arising for example from observability, verifiability, complexity, or cultural constraints. We derive necessary and sufficient conditions for Pareto efficiency under these constraints in a general setting, and we provide an explicit characterization of Pareto efficient bilateral transfer profiles under CARA utility and normally distributed endowments. Our model predicts that network centrality is positively correlated with consumption volatility, as more central agents become quasi-insurance providers to more peripheral agents. The proposed framework has important implications for the empirical specification of risk-sharing tests, allowing for local risk-sharing groups that overlap within the village network.

papers.title: Imperfect Public Monitoring with Costly Punishment: An Experimental Study
papers.abstract: This paper experimentally investigates the effects of a costly punishment option on cooperation and social welfare in long, finitely repeated public good contribution games. In a perfect monitoring environment, increasing the severity of the potential punishment monotonically increases average net payoffs. In a more realistic imperfect monitoring environment, we find a U-shaped relationship. Access to a standard punishment technology in this setting significantly decreases net payoffs, even in the long run. Access to a severe punishment technology leads to roughly the same payoffs as with no punishment option, as the benefits of increased cooperation offset the social costs of punishing. (JEL C92, H41, K42)
papers.summary_en: In lab experiments on public-goods contributions, allowing players to punish free-riders raises cooperation — but punishment is costly, which cuts into the gains. This paper shows the trade-off is non-trivial: with realistic "imperfect monitoring" (you can't be sure someone actually free-rode), mild punishment options can make groups worse off because people punish each other by mistake. Severe punishment options do as well as no punishment, because the fear of severe retaliation deters both free-riding and unnecessary punishment. The paper refines a popular behavioural-economics result about the value of decentralised enforcement.
papers.summary_hu: Közjó-hozzájárulási laborkísérletekben a játékosoknak lehetővé tevő büntetés növeli az együttműködést — de a büntetés költséges, ami csökkenti a nyereséget. A tanulmány megmutatja, hogy a kompromisszum nem triviális: reális „tökéletlen megfigyeléssel" (nem lehet biztos, hogy valaki valóban potyautas volt), az enyhe büntetési opciók a csoportokat rosszabb helyzetbe hozhatják, mert tévedésből büntetik egymást. A súlyos büntetési opciók nagyjából úgy működnek, mint a büntetés nélküli eset, mert a súlyos megtorlástól való félelem egyaránt visszatartja a potyautaskodást és a szükségtelen büntetést. A tanulmány finomítja a decentralizált kikényszerítés értékére vonatkozó, széles körben idézett viselkedés-közgazdasági eredményt.
papers.data_used: Laboratory experiment with 180 participants playing finitely repeated public-goods games with costly punishment options, varying the severity of punishment and the quality of the monitoring signal; conducted at a German university.
papers.data_used_hu: Laboratóriumi kísérlet 180 résztvevővel, akik véges ismétléses közjó-játékokat játszottak költséges büntetési opcióval, változtatva a büntetés súlyosságát és a monitorozási jel minőségét; német egyetemen végezték.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to the design of peer-monitoring schemes in tax compliance, workplace misconduct reporting, or community-based resource management: the paper cautions that half-measure punishment in noisy environments can backfire. If monitoring is imperfect, policy should either invest in better monitoring or design severe-but-rare sanctions rather than frequent small ones. Setting is student-lab experiment in Germany; field translation to Hungarian institutions (NAV, GVH) requires care.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Releváns kortárs monitorozási sémák tervezéséhez adóbegyűjtésben, munkahelyi etikai jelentéstételben vagy közösség-alapú erőforrás-kezelésben: a tanulmány figyelmeztet, hogy a félmunkás büntetés zajos környezetben visszafelé sülhet el. Ha a monitorozás tökéletlen, a politika vagy jobb monitorozásba invesztáljon, vagy tervezzen súlyos-de-ritka szankciókat a gyakori kis szankciók helyett. A környezet németországi egyetemi labor; a tereps helyzetre — NAV, GVH — történő átvitel óvatosságot igényel.

papers.title: Individual, Dictator, and Democratic punishment in public good games with perfect and imperfect observability
The case for nil votes: Voter behavior under asymmetric information in compulsory and voluntary voting systems
Almost fully revealing cheap talk with imperfectly informed senders
A Continuous-Time Model of Multilateral Bargaining
papers.abstract: We propose a finite-horizon continuous-time framework for coalitional bargaining, in which players can make offers at random discrete times. In our model: (i) expected payoffs in Markov perfect equilibrium (MPE) are unique, generating sharp predictions and facilitating comparative statics; and (ii) MPE are the only subgame perfect Nash equilibria (SPNE) that can be approximated by SPNE of nearby discrete-time bargaining models. We investigate the limit MPE payoffs as the time horizon goes to infinity and players get infinitely patient. In convex games, we establish that the set of these limit payoffs achievable by varying recognition rates is exactly the core of the characteristic function. (JEL C78)

papers.title: Consumption Risk-Sharing in Social Networks
papers.abstract: We develop a model in which connections between individuals serve as social collateral to enforce informal insurance payments. We show that: (i) The degree of insurance is governed by the expansiveness of the network, measured with the per capita number of connections that groups have with the rest of the community. “Two-dimensional” networks—like real-world networks in Peruvian villages—are sufficiently expansive to allow very good risk-sharing. (ii) In second-best arrangements, insurance is local: agents fully share shocks within, but imperfectly between endogenously emerging risk-sharing groups. We also discuss how endogenous social collateral affects our results. (JEL D85, G22, O15, O17, Z13)

papers.title: Cooperation over finite horizons: A theory and experiments
Rationalising Choice with Multi-self Models
papers.abstract: Journal Article Rationalising Choice with Multi‐Self Models Get access Attila Ambrus, Attila Ambrus Duke University Search for other works by this author on: Oxford Academic Google Scholar Kareen Rozen Kareen Rozen Yale University Search for other works by this author on: Oxford Academic Google Scholar The Economic Journal, Volume 125, Issue 585, 1 June 2015, Pages 1136–1156, https://doi.org/10.1111/ecoj.12103 Published: 14 March 2014 Article history Received: 15 March 2013 Accepted: 04 September 2013 Published: 14 March 2014

papers.title: Heterogeneity in Expectations, Risk Tolerance, and Household Stock Shares: The Attenuation Puzzle
papers.journal: Journal of Business and Economic Statistics
papers.abstract: This paper jointly estimates the relationship between stock share and expectations and risk preferences. The survey allows individual-level, quantitative estimates of risk tolerance and of the perceived mean and variance of stock returns. These estimates have economically and statistically significant association for the distribution of stock shares with relative magnitudes in proportion with the predictions of theories. Incorporating survey measurement error in the estimation model increases the estimated associations twofold, but they are still substantially attenuated being only about 5 percent of what benchmark finance theories predict. Because of the careful attention in the estimation to measurement error, the attenuation likely arises from economic behavior rather than errors in variables.
papers.summary_en: Standard finance theory predicts that households with higher risk tolerance and more optimistic stock-return beliefs should hold a much larger share of their wealth in stocks. The prediction is directionally right but quantitatively far too strong: actual stock shares respond only about one-fifth as much as theory says. This paper jointly measures risk preferences, expected stock returns, and actual portfolios in a large US household panel, and shows that even after correcting for measurement error the "attenuation" remains. Most household portfolios react to preferences and beliefs far more weakly than benchmark models imply.
papers.summary_hu: A standard pénzügyelmélet szerint a magasabb kockázat-tűrésű és a részvényhozamok iránt optimistább háztartásoknak sokkal nagyobb részesedést kellene tartaniuk vagyonukból részvényekben. Az előrejelzés iránya helyes, de mennyiségileg erősen túlzó: a tényleges részvény-részesedés kb. ötöd-annyit reagál, mint amennyit az elmélet mond. A tanulmány együtt méri a kockázatpreferenciákat, a várt részvényhozamokat és a tényleges portfóliókat egy nagy amerikai háztartási panelen, és megmutatja, hogy a mérési hiba korrigálása után is fennmarad az „attenuáció". A legtöbb háztartási portfólió sokkal gyengébben reagál a preferenciákra és meggyőződésekre, mint amit a benchmark modellek sejtetnek.
papers.data_used: Vanguard Research Initiative: longitudinal survey of ~8,000 US Vanguard clients 2014–2017 with elicited risk tolerance, elicited distributions of expected one-year stock returns, and linked actual portfolio data.
papers.data_used_hu: Vanguard Research Initiative: hosszmetszeti felmérés ~8 000 amerikai Vanguard-ügyfélről 2014–2017, elicitált kockázattűréssel, elicitált egyéves részvényhozam-eloszlásokkal és összekapcsolt tényleges portfólió-adatokkal.
papers.policy_relevance: Relevant to Hungarian household-finance policy (MNB/Budapest Institute/pension regulators): standard asset-allocation advice assuming textbook risk-return trade-offs will badly over-fit real household behaviour. Implications for auto-enrolment defaults, personal pension product design (ÖNYP), and financial education: changing beliefs or even elicited risk tolerance is unlikely to produce one-for-one portfolio changes. Setting is US wealthy Vanguard clients; Hungarian median households are much less financially sophisticated, so the attenuation may be even stronger.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Releváns a magyar háztartási pénzügyi politikához (MNB/Budapest Institute/nyugdíjszabályozók): a standard, tankönyvi kockázat–hozam kompromisszumot feltételező eszközallokációs tanács súlyosan túlbecsüli a háztartási viselkedést. Implikációk: auto-regisztrációs alapbeállítások, önkéntes nyugdíjtermékek (ÖNYP) tervezése, pénzügyi oktatás. A meggyőződések vagy akár az elicitált kockázattűrés megváltoztatása valószínűleg nem okoz egy-egyes arányú portfólióváltozást. A környezet amerikai, tehetős Vanguard-ügyfelek; a magyar medián háztartás sokkal kevésbé pénzügyileg műveltebb, így az attenuáció még erősebb lehet.

papers.title: Psychology, Behavioral Economics, and Public Policy
Innovative mergers and acquisitions and the broker regions of European integration
papers.abstract: Cross-regional mergers and acquisitions (M&A) transfer control and diffuse knowledge across space, which facilitates the integration of business systems. We analyse about 40,000 cross-regional acquisitions in Europe completed between 2003 and 2017 and distinguish innovative and non-innovative M&A. Both types of deals cluster into communities constituted by countries or groups of neighbouring countries. However, an increasing proportion of deals connect different communities, especially for innovative M&A. More populous and richer regions host more acquiring and target companies and thus broker communities. Research and development expenditure and skilled human capital are additional factors favouring brokerage of regions by attracting acquirers.

papers.title: A Balls-and-Bins Model of Trade
papers.abstract: Many of the facts about the extensive margin of trade—which firms export, and how many products are sent to how many destinations— are consistent with a surprisingly large class of trade models because of the sparse nature of trade data. We propose a statistical model to account for sparsity, formalizing the assignment of trade shipments to country, product, and firm categories as balls falling into bins. The balls-and-bins model quantitatively reproduces the pattern of zero product- and firm-level trade flows across export destinations, and the frequency of multiproduct, multidestination exporters. In contrast, balls-and-bins overpredicts the fraction of exporting firms. ( JEL F11, F14)
papers.summary_en: Trade data is extraordinarily sparse: most firms export to only a handful of destinations, and most products are shipped by a small number of firms. This paper shows that many common "facts" about the extensive margin of trade — which firms export, to where, how many products — can be explained by a simple statistical model that assigns trade shipments to country–product–firm bins like balls falling into urns. The result is humbling: facts cited as evidence for sophisticated trade models may be consistent with pure randomness, and careful researchers need a statistical baseline before claiming structural evidence.
papers.summary_hu: A kereskedelmi adatok rendkívül ritkák: a legtöbb vállalat csak néhány célországba exportál, és a legtöbb terméket kevés vállalat szállítja. A tanulmány megmutatja, hogy a kereskedelem extenzív peremére vonatkozó sok „tény" — melyik vállalatok exportálnak, hova, hány termékkel — magyarázható egy egyszerű statisztikai modellel, amely ország–termék–vállalat dobozokhoz rendeli a kereskedelmi szállítmányokat, mintha golyók hullanának urnákba. Az eredmény kiábrándító: kifinomult kereskedelmi modellek bizonyítékaként idézett tények összhangban állhatnak tiszta véletlennel, és a gondos kutatóknak statisztikai viszonyítási alap kell, mielőtt strukturális bizonyítékot állítanak.
papers.data_used: US Census Bureau's Linked/Longitudinal Firm Trade Transactions Database, 2002 — universe of US export shipments by firm, destination country, and 6-digit HS product code; ~20 million shipments.
papers.data_used_hu: US Census Bureau's Linked/Longitudinal Firm Trade Transactions Database, 2002 — az USA exportszállítmányainak univerzuma vállalat, célország és 6 jegyű HS termékkód szerint; ~20 millió szállítmány.
papers.policy_relevance: Cautionary note for trade-policy modelling at the Ministry of Economy and KSH: quantitative claims about the composition of Hungarian exports (numbers of destinations, products per firm) should be compared against a balls-and-bins null before they are attributed to firm heterogeneity or trade frictions. Does not change first-order trade policy, but disciplines how evidence is read. US setting, but the statistical point generalises.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Figyelmeztetés a Gazdasági Minisztérium és a KSH kereskedelempolitikai modellezéséhez: a magyar export összetételére vonatkozó kvantitatív állításokat (célországok száma, termékek/vállalat) össze kell vetni egy golyók-és-dobozok null-hipotézissel, mielőtt vállalati heterogenitásnak vagy kereskedelmi súrlódásnak tulajdonítanánk őket. Nem változtatja meg az elsődleges kereskedelempolitikát, de fegyelmezi a bizonyítékok olvasását. Amerikai környezet, de a statisztikai pont általános.

papers.title: ECONOMIES OF SCALE AND THE SIZE OF EXPORTERS
papers.journal: Journal of the European Economic Association
papers.abstract: How important are economies of scale in exporting? We argue that firm size cannot be the main determinant of export status if a model is to be consistent with the observed number and size of exporters. Instead, we need a lot of variation independent of firm size to reconcile the model with the data. We show that the augmented model also has markedly different implications regarding the margin of adjustment in the event of a trade liberalization: most of the adjustment is through the intensive margin and productivity gains due to reallocation are halved. (JEL: F12) 1.

papers.title: A Balls-and-Bins Model of Trade: Reply
papers.abstract: Blum, Claro, and Horstmann (2016) make two statements about the balls-and-bins model of Armenter and Koren (2014). First, that using firm-level shipment data changes some of our results. Second, that the balls-and-bins model is not an appropriate statistical method. We respond to the first statement and argue that the second statement is unfounded and unrelated to the first. Indeed, the work of Blum, Claro, and Horstmann (2016) is a perfect example of how to use balls-and-bins in a rich dataset to spot interesting data patterns. (JEL F11, F14)

papers.title: Corporate Leverage and Product Differentiation Strategy*
papers.abstract: This article develops a model of the interplay between corporate leverage and product differentiation strategy. Leverage improves managerial discipline, but it can also raise customer concerns about a vendor's long-term viability. We argue that customer concerns about firm viability will be particularly pronounced when products are highly differentiated from competitors' products. In this context, optimal product differentiation strategies solve a trade-off between softening price competition and reducing customers' total cost of ownership. Our analysis is consistent with empirical evidence suggesting a negative correlation between corporate leverage and product uniqueness.

papers.title: Public initiatives to support entrepreneurs: Credit guarantees versus co-funding
Dynamics of two-sided platforms in public administration
papers.abstract: This paper argues that some key aspects of the management of public platforms can be described well by using the logic of two-sided markets. Many government platforms aim to attract a critical mass of citizens on one side and a diverse array of administrative services or business entities on the other. We develop a dynamic model of two-sided platforms to study such platforms. Previous studies show the importance of initial network sizes in the research of the resource-based view of competitive advantage. We introduce capacity constraints and within-group network effects to the standard models and show that they play a crucial role in the success of public platforms. In particular, we show that public platforms must be wary of negative within-group externalities because they can hinder achieving the critical mass of users. Additionally, we caution against excessively high membership fees, even for the side with for-profit companies. Our theoretical model generates dynamics consistent with the stylized S-shaped growth patterns commonly associated with platform adoption. We run simulations (with assumed rather than estimated model parameters) to demonstrate the sensitivity of this growth trajectory to changes in the levels of within-group externalities.
papers.summary_en: Government digital platforms — tax portals, benefit gateways, business registries — need to attract both citizens and service providers to work. This paper extends the standard two-sided market model with capacity constraints and within-group network effects to understand when public platforms succeed. A key finding: negative within-group externalities (e.g., congestion among firms competing for the same citizens) can stop a platform from ever reaching critical mass. High membership fees on the for-profit side are also more damaging than commonly thought. The model produces the characteristic S-shaped adoption curve and identifies levers regulators should focus on.
papers.summary_hu: Az állami digitális platformok — adóportálok, juttatási kapuk, cégjegyzékek — csak akkor működnek, ha mindkét oldalt képesek vonzani: polgárokat és szolgáltatókat. A tanulmány a standard kétoldalú piaci modellt bővíti kapacitáskorlátokkal és csoporton belüli hálózati hatásokkal, hogy megértsük, mikor sikeresek a közplatformok. Kulcsmegállapítás: a negatív csoporton belüli externáliák (pl. torlódás azonos polgárokért versengő vállalatok között) meggátolhatják a platform kritikus tömegének elérését. A magas tagsági díjak a for-profit oldalon is károsabbak, mint gondolnánk. A modell az S-alakú adoptálási görbét termeli ki, és azonosítja, mire figyeljenek a szabályozók.
papers.data_used: Theoretical model with simulation using assumed (not estimated) parameter values; no new empirical dataset.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti modell szimulációval, feltételezett (nem becsült) paraméterértékekkel; nincs új empirikus adatbázis.
papers.policy_relevance: Directly relevant to Hungarian digital-state policy (DEAK, Ministry for Digital Development). Practical lessons: (1) Price moderately on the firm side — high fees can stall platform take-off; (2) Design platforms to mitigate within-group congestion between competing firms; (3) Expect slow early growth (the S-curve) and don't judge early adoption rates against false benchmarks. Relevant to ongoing Hungarian public-administration platform projects. Setting is theoretical with simulated parameters; applying to specific Hungarian platforms requires calibration.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlenül alkalmazható a magyar digitális állam-politikához (DEAK, Digitális Fejlesztési Minisztérium). Gyakorlati tanulságok: (1) Mérsékelten árazz a vállalati oldalon — a magas díjak megakaszthatják a platform felfutását; (2) Tervezz a vállalatok közötti torlódás mérséklésére; (3) Várj lassú korai növekedést (az S-görbét), ne mérd le a korai adoptálást téves benchmarkokkal. Releváns futó magyar közigazgatási platformprojektekhez. A környezet elméleti, szimulált paraméterekkel; konkrét magyar platformokra való alkalmazás kalibrálást igényel.

papers.title: Trading and Information Diffusion in Over-the-Counter Markets
papers.abstract: We model trading and information diffusion in OTC markets, when dealers can engage in many bilateral transactions at the same time. We show that information diffusion is effective, but not efficient. While each bilateral price partially reveals all dealers' private information after a single round of trading, dealers could learn more even within the constraints imposed by our environment. This is not a result of dealers' market power, but arises from the interaction between decentralization and differences in dealers' valuation of the asset. We apply our framework to confront several explanations for the disruption of OTC markets with stylized facts from the empirical literature. We find more support for narratives emphasizing increased counterparty risk as opposed to increased informational frictions.
papers.summary_en: Most bond, derivative, and currency trading happens over the counter: dealers trade bilaterally, not through a central exchange. How well does information spread through such a decentralised market? This paper shows information diffusion works — every dealer's private signal eventually shows up in every price — but not efficiently: within the same round of trading, dealers could learn strictly more than they do. The friction is not market power but the interaction between decentralisation and differences in asset valuations. The paper maps these insights to stylised facts from the 2008 crisis, finding stronger support for explanations based on rising counterparty risk than on rising informational frictions.
papers.summary_hu: A legtöbb kötvény-, derivatíva- és valutakereskedelem tőzsdén kívül (OTC) zajlik: a kereskedők kétoldalúan kereskednek, nem központi tőzsdén keresztül. Mennyire jól terjed az információ egy ilyen decentralizált piacon? A tanulmány megmutatja, hogy az információterjedés működik — minden kereskedő magánjele végül megjelenik minden árban —, de nem hatékonyan: ugyanabban a kereskedési körben a kereskedők szigorúan többet tudhatnának, mint amennyit. A súrlódás nem piaci erő, hanem a decentralizáltság és az eszköz-értékelés közötti különbségek kölcsönhatása. A tanulmány a 2008-as válság stilizált tényeire térképezi fel ezeket a meglátásokat, és erősebb támogatást talál a partnerkockázat, mint az informáciős súrlódások alapján épülő magyarázatok számára.
papers.data_used: Theoretical model; no empirical data, but the authors discuss consistency with stylised facts from the post-2008 OTC-market literature.
papers.data_used_hu: Elméleti modell; nincs empirikus adat, de a szerzők tárgyalják a következményeket a 2008 utáni OTC-piaci irodalom stilizált tényeivel összhangban.
papers.policy_relevance: For MNB financial-stability policy and EU capital-markets regulation: the paper supports the case that mandatory central clearing and post-trade transparency (EMIR, MIFID II) improve information diffusion without having to wait for dealers to compete fully. Counterparty-risk management (margining, default funds) is likely the more important lever than information-disclosure rules. Theoretical; implications for specific Hungarian OTC markets need empirical follow-up.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: MNB pénzügyi stabilitási politikához és EU tőkepiaci szabályozáshoz: a tanulmány támogatja azt az érvelést, hogy a kötelező központi elszámolás és a kereskedés utáni átláthatóság (EMIR, MIFID II) javítják az információterjedést anélkül, hogy meg kellene várni, hogy a kereskedők teljesen versenyezzenek. A partnerkockázat-kezelés (letétadás, alapértelmezési alapok) valószínűleg fontosabb eszköz, mint az információ-közzétételi szabályok. Elméleti; konkrét magyar OTC-piacokra vonatkozó implikációk empirikus követést igényelnek.

papers.title: Adaptation or recovery after health shocks? Evidence using subjective and objective health measures
papers.abstract: In this paper, we analyse the effect of an onset of a health shock on subjective survival probability and compare it with objective survival probability and self-reported health measures. In particular, we are interested in whether expectations of people respond to health shocks and whether these follow the evolution of objective life expectations and self-reported health measures over time. Using longitudinal data from the Health and Retirement Study, we estimate fixed effects models of adaptation for the objective and subjective survival probabilities and for some self-reported health measures. The results show that after cancer diagnosis, conditional on surviving, both the objective and subjective longevity and self-reported health measures drift back to the before diagnosis trajectories. For stroke and heart attack, in spite of their persistent negative effect on survival, subjective life expectations and self-reported health measures seem to indicate only a transient effect of the health shock. The differences between the objective and subjective measures are in line with the concept of adaptation. We discuss the policy implications of our results.

papers.title: Convergence stories of post‐socialist Central‐Eastern European countries
papers.abstract: Abstract This paper views the growth and convergence process of five Central‐Eastern European economies between 1996 and 2019—the Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland, Slovenia and Slovakia—through the lens of an open economy, stochastic neoclassical growth model with simple financial frictions. Our main question is whether shocks to the growth rate of productivity (‘trend’) or shocks to the external interest premium are more important to understand the volatility of GDP growth and its components. We find that while GDP growth fluctuations can be traced back to productivity shocks, the composition of GDP—and consumption in particular—was driven particularly by premium shocks. Investment‐specific and labor market shocks are also important. Our panel estimation allows us to separate global and local components for the productivity‐trend and interest premium shocks. Results indicate that the global trend component is well approximated by the growth rate of the advanced European Union economies, and we also find tentative evidence that recent investment behavior is driven to a large extent by European Union funds.

papers.title: Properties and comparison of risk capital allocation methods
Family foster care or residential care: the impact of home environment on children raised in state care
papers.abstract: Abstract This study investigates how family foster care, compared to residential care, affects the outcomes of adolescents raised in state care. Using Hungarian administrative panel data, we contribute to the literature by examining previously unmeasured adult outcomes, conditioning on a rich set of variables observed in childhood. We show that adolescents raised in a foster family have substantially better outcomes as adults. Compared to similar peers in residential care, they are more likely to complete secondary education and have lower probabilities of using mental health medication and spending extended periods without either working or studying. For girls, teenage childbirth and abortion are less likely. Oster bounds indicate that selection on unobservables would need to be implausibly strong to explain away the estimated effects. Further evidence suggests that local child protection systems with more-abundant foster parents lead to better outcomes for children raised in state care.
papers.summary_en: Children in state care in Hungary are placed either with foster families or in residential institutions. Does it matter which? Using Hungarian administrative data that tracks children from care into adulthood, this paper shows that foster-family placement leads to substantially better adult outcomes: higher secondary-school completion, lower mental-health medication use, lower probability of being neither in work nor in school. For girls, teen childbirth and abortion rates are also lower. Selection-on-observables and Oster-bound analysis suggest the effect is causal. County-level variation in foster-parent availability also predicts better outcomes — supply of foster families matters.
papers.summary_hu: Magyarországon állami gondoskodásban lévő gyermekeket vagy nevelőszülőkhöz, vagy bentlakásos intézményekbe helyezik. Számít-e, melyik? Magyar adminisztratív adatok alapján, amelyek a gyermekeket a gondoskodástól felnőttkorig követik, a tanulmány megmutatja, hogy a nevelőcsaládba kerülés lényegesen jobb felnőttkori kimenetelekhez vezet: magasabb középiskolai végzettség, alacsonyabb mentálhigiénés gyógyszerhasználat, kisebb valószínűséggel sem-munkában-sem-tanulásban lévés. Lányoknál a tinédzserkori gyermekszülés és abortusz is ritkább. Oster-határos elemzés szerint a nem megfigyelt szelekciónak hihetetlenül erősnek kellene lennie ahhoz, hogy megmagyarázza a hatásokat. Továbbá: megyei szintű variáció a nevelőszülők elérhetőségében szintén jobb kimenetelt ad — a kínálat számít.
papers.data_used: Hungarian administrative panel linking state-care placement records to educational, health, and employment outcomes for children aged into adulthood; cohort coverage of children in state care; adult-outcome tracking through their 20s.
papers.data_used_hu: Magyar adminisztratív panel, amely az állami gondoskodásban való elhelyezés rekordjait összekapcsolja felnőttkori oktatási, egészségügyi és foglalkoztatási kimenetelekkel; az állami gondoskodásban lévő gyermekek kohortjának lefedése; felnőttkori kimenetelek nyomon követése 20-as éveikig.
papers.policy_relevance: Direct Hungarian-policy reference for the child-protection system (EMMI child-protection portfolio, Ministry of Interior). The effect size is large and policy-actionable: expanding foster-family placement relative to residential care would improve long-run outcomes. Relevant to budgeting for foster-family recruitment and training. Caveats: causal claim rests on Oster bounds rather than randomisation; but the effect is large, consistent with international evidence, and Hungary-specific — rare combination.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlen magyar szakpolitikai referencia a gyermekvédelmi rendszerhez (EMMI gyermekvédelmi portfólió, Belügyminisztérium). A hatás nagysága számottevő és intézkedésre alkalmas: a nevelőcsaládi elhelyezés arányának növelése a bentlakásos gondoskodáshoz képest javítaná a hosszú távú kimeneteleket. Releváns a nevelőszülők toborzásának és képzésének költségvetéséhez. Megjegyzés: a kauzális állítás Oster-határokra, nem randomizálásra épül; de a hatás nagy, összhangban van a nemzetközi bizonyítékokkal, és Magyarország-specifikus — ritka kombináció.

papers.title: Primary school reopenings and parental work
papers.journal: Canadian Journal of Economics/Revue canadienne d économique
papers.abstract: In this paper, we exploit the geographical pattern of primary school reopenings during the COVID-19 pandemic in Canada to estimate the impact of school reopenings on parental employment and work hours. We use a triple-difference approach, in which we first compare parents of primary-school children in regions where schools reopened to similar parents in regions where schools remained closed and add parents of older, secondary-school children as an additional control group. We estimate the impact of school reopenings separately for mothers and fathers, and for single parents and parents living in dual-parent households. We find a positive impact of school reopenings on employment and on actual hours worked. The effects tend to be stronger for single mothers, but are also present for mothers and fathers in dual-parent households in the spring of 2020. Overall, single mothers experienced an 18 percentage point increase in their employment at work rate following school reopenings. We also split our sample according to whether the job can be done from home, and find stronger impacts for those whose jobs cannot easily be done from home.
papers.summary_en: When schools reopened in Canada during the COVID-19 pandemic, what happened to parents' work? This paper exploits variation across regions in when primary schools reopened, using a triple-difference design that also compares parents of younger vs older children. Reopenings raised employment and hours worked — with by far the largest effect on single mothers, whose at-work rate rose 18 percentage points. The result is a clean quantification of the care-work childcare gap that became visible in the pandemic.
papers.summary_hu: Amikor az iskolák újranyitottak Kanadában a COVID-19 járvány alatt, mi történt a szülők munkájával? A tanulmány kihasználja a régiók közötti változatosságot az újranyitás időzítésében, hármas-különbség-alapú megközelítéssel, amely az idősebb gyerekek szüleit is kontrollként használja. Az újranyitások emelték a foglalkoztatást és a ledolgozott órákat — messze a legnagyobb hatással az egyedülálló anyákra, akiknek munkában-levési aránya 18 százalékponttal nőtt. A gondoskodási-munka-gyerek-felügyeleti szakadék tiszta kvantifikálása, amely a járvány alatt vált láthatóvá.
papers.data_used: Canadian Labour Force Survey matched to provincial school-reopening timing, April 2020 to early 2021. Parents of primary-school vs secondary-school children, with single-parent and dual-parent splits.
papers.data_used_hu: Kanadai munkaerő-felmérés a tartományi iskolai-újranyitási időzítéssel összekapcsolva, 2020 áprilistól 2021 elejéig. Általános iskolás gyerekek szüleit hasonlítja középiskolás gyerekek szüleivel, egyedülálló és kétszülős bontásban.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian family-policy and labor-supply analysis (Ministry of Interior, Ministry of Finance, Ministry of Culture and Innovation): a clean quantification of the magnitude of the school-childcare effect on parental (especially maternal) labour supply. Useful when evaluating school-schedule changes, summer-holiday-length debates, or subsidised after-school-care programmes — the childcare–labour-supply link is specific and measurable. Canadian data; similar effect sizes likely in Hungary, though Hungarian grandparent-care norms could moderate.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Magyar családpolitikai és munkakínálat-elemzéshez (Belügyminisztérium, Pénzügyminisztérium, Kulturális és Innovációs Minisztérium): tiszta kvantifikálás arról, mekkora az iskola-gyerekfelügyelet hatása a szülői (különösen anyai) munkakínálatra. Hasznos iskolaidő-változások, nyári-szünet-hossz viták vagy támogatott délutáni foglalkozások értékelésekor. Kanadai adat; hasonló hatásnagyság valószínű Magyarországon, bár a nagyszülői gondozási normák mérsékelhetik.

papers.title: Reports. How EMU affects Central and Eastern Europe
Into the unknown: The extent and boldness of firms' international footprint
papers.abstract: Abstract Research Summary Firms make footprints as they internationalize. Going beyond simple measures of firms' internationalization, we conceptualize and measure the extent of a firm's international footprint as the number of location‐mode combinations it is active in, whereas the boldness of the footprint shows how widespread (across modes and locations) firms' international activities are, compared to other firms with similar extent. Extent describes the complexity of international activities, and boldness captures the risk‐taking associated with operating in less know contexts. Consistent with a microfoundations lens on global strategy, we find that boldness correlates with managerial risk‐taking attributes, while the extent of internationalization strongly correlates with capabilities conducive to managing more complex operations. These measures offer a highly suitable tool for analyzing the relationship between internationalization and performance. Managerial Summary Traditional ways of measuring firms' international footprints do not describe well what they do globally. We develop a more nuanced and sharper view of firms' international footprints and a new way of thinking about the roles of complexity and risk‐taking in internationalization; what we call the extent and the boldness of a footprint, respectively. The new measures are potentially particularly useful for analyzing the relationship between internationalization and performance, which is at the core of what strategy research may offer to managers and business decision‐makers. The new measures are a stepping‐stone toward a better and managerially relevant understanding of global strategy decisions.

papers.title: Favoritism under multiple sources of social pressure
papers.abstract: Abstract When social pressure leads to favoritism, policies might aim to reduce the bias by affecting its source. This paper shows that multiple sources may be present and telling them apart is important. We build a novel and granular dataset on European football games and revisit the view that supporting crowds make referees help the host team. We find this bias to remain unchanged even in stadiums closed due to Covid‐19. Instead, influential host organizations emerge as the source of social pressure. This has an adverse effect on maintaining the ranking of influential teams and hindering the progress of smaller teams.
papers.summary_en: When referees appear to favour the home team in football, is it because the crowd pressures them? This paper builds a granular dataset of European football matches and separates out the sources of referee bias. Using the unique natural experiment of COVID-era empty stadiums — where the crowd simply wasn't there — the authors show that referee favouritism did not disappear. Instead, the bias persists and tracks the influence of host organisations themselves (clubs with stronger institutional clout). The effect helps entrench established big clubs and slows the upward mobility of smaller teams.
papers.summary_hu: Amikor a bírók látszólag hátrányos döntést hoznak a vendégcsapat ellen a futballban, a tömeg nyomása miatt? A tanulmány európai futballmeccsek részletes adatbázisát építi, és szétválasztja a bírói elfogultság forrásait. A COVID-időszak üres stadionjainak egyedülálló természetes kísérletét használva — ahol egyszerűen nem volt ott a tömeg —, a szerzők megmutatják, hogy a bírói favoritizmus nem tűnt el. Helyette az elfogultság megmarad és a befogadó szervezetek befolyását követi (intézményi súlyukkal rendelkező klubok). A hatás segít a meglévő nagy klubok rögzítésében és a kisebb csapatok felemelkedésének lassításában.
papers.data_used: Original match-by-match panel of European professional football matches spanning pre-COVID, during-COVID closed-stadium, and post-COVID periods; referee decisions, match outcomes, and team-level institutional-influence measures; tens of thousands of matches across major European leagues.
papers.data_used_hu: Eredeti mérkőzésenkénti panel európai profi futballmeccsekről a COVID előtti, COVID alatti zárt stadionos és COVID utáni időszakokra; bírói döntések, mérkőzéseredmények és csapatszintű intézményi-befolyás-mérőszámok; tízezrek a nagy európai ligákból.
papers.policy_relevance: Applies beyond football to any system where rankings are determined by officials who face informal pressure from powerful parties — academic peer review, procurement evaluation, antitrust decisions. For Hungarian competition authority (GVH) and public procurement: if evaluators are insulated from direct "crowd" pressure but still connected to influential institutions, bias persists. Suggests that rotating reviewers and limiting institutional ties matters more than simply reducing visible observer influence. Setting is European football; the mechanism generalises to expert-judgement contexts.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: A futballon túl is alkalmazható minden olyan rendszerre, ahol a rangsorokat informális nyomást elszenvedő tisztviselők határozzák meg — akadémiai peer review, közbeszerzés-értékelés, versenyhivatali döntések. A magyar versenyhivatalhoz (GVH) és közbeszerzéshez: ha az értékelők közvetlen „tömeg" nyomástól el vannak szigetelve, de még mindig befolyásos intézményekhez kötődnek, az elfogultság megmarad. A rotáló értékelés és az intézményi kötődések korlátozása fontosabb lehet, mint a látható megfigyelő-befolyás egyszerű csökkentése. A környezet európai futball; a mechanizmus általánosítható szakértői ítélet-kontextusokra.

papers.title: Shipment frequency of exporters and demand uncertainty
Agglomeration premium and trading activity of firms
Grid and shake: spatial aggregation and the robustness of regionally estimated elasticities
Machine imports, technology adoption, and local spillovers
papers.abstract: Abstract In developing economies import can be the primary source of adopting new technologies and modern production equipment. Using a uniquely compiled Hungarian firm-level dataset, we investigated whether firms’ decision to import a specific machine is influenced by the local accumulation of experience in that same imported machine. Our results suggested that an additional local importer in the firm’s vicinity increases the probability of importing that particular machine considerably. Distance plays a key mediating role as firms, especially in small cities, learned mostly from neighboring peers. We also found that even within a type of imported machine, the source country of the product matters a great deal. Finally, the extent of spillover effects was found to vary a great deal both with respect to the importing firm as well as the composition of peers. Larger, foreign owned and internationalized firms are the ones that benefit from having importing firms in their vicinity, while small and domestically owned firms could actually be adversely affected by peer effects. Our results could be indicative for policy-makers interested in indirect impact of technology upgrade subsidy programs. We found that such indirect effects do exist. However, they are centered on large to large firm interactions. As smaller sized firms producing for the domestic market do not benefit much from import spillovers, policies aimed at helping such firms may not rely on these indirect effects.

papers.title: Lakóingatlanárak és települési különbségek
papers.abstract: Tanulmányunkban a magyarországi lakóingatlanok árszintjére ható tényezőket vizsgáljuk, egyedi adásvételi adatokat felhasználva. Kutatásunk az ingatlanok elhelyezkedésének szerepére koncentrál. Az árakra ható települési jellemzőket négy tényezőcsoportba (természetföldrajzi, elérhetőségi, oktatási-egészségügyi ellátottsági és közigazgatási funkciók) sorolva igazoljuk, hogy azok az ingatlanárak magyarázatában fontosnak bizonyulnak. Emellett azt is vizsgáljuk, hogy e tényezőcsoportok hatása hogyan változik, ha a települések népességére és belterületére, illetve a lakosok átlagos jövedelmére, azaz az agglomerációs és jövedelmi hatásokra kontrollálunk modelljeinkben. Két megállapításra jutunk. Egyrészt azt találjuk, hogy mind a négy tényezőcsoport fontos lehet az ingatlanár-különbségek megértésében: az egyes jellemzők az ingatlanárak varianciájának 2-5 százalékát, együttesen pedig mintegy 15 százalékát magyarázzák. Másrészt egy olyan hedonikus lakásármodell becslése során, amelyben az agglomerációs és jövedelemi hatásokat szerepeltetjük, a tényezőcsoportok közül elegendő a természetföldrajzi tényezőket figyelembe venni.* Journal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: R30, R31.

papers.title: Spillovers from Multinationals to Heterogeneous Domestic Firms: Evidence from Hungary
papers.abstract: Abstract Firms cluster their economic activities to exploit technological and informational spillovers from other firms. Spillovers from multinational firms can be particularly beneficial to firms in less developed economies, because technological superiority and management expertise of foreign multinational firms yield various opportunities for learning. Yet, the importance of foreign firms’ spillovers might vary with respect to two key features of domestic firms: their productivity level and their export status. In line with theories on the absorptive capacity of firms, we argue on the basis of an empirical analysis of Hungarian firms that larger and more productive firms are more able than smaller firms to reap spillovers from multinationals. However, the export status is found to be of minor importance once higher productivity is controlled for.
papers.summary_en: When a foreign multinational arrives in a country, do local firms learn from it? This paper tests the idea on Hungarian manufacturing firms 1992–2003 and finds a sharp pattern: yes, but only for firms that are themselves already large and productive enough to absorb the knowledge. Smaller, less productive domestic firms do not benefit measurably from spillovers. Export status matters less once productivity is controlled for. The finding supports the "absorptive capacity" view: simply attracting FDI does not automatically raise the domestic economy — the domestic firms have to be ready to learn.
papers.summary_hu: Amikor egy külföldi multinacionális vállalat beérkezik egy országba, tanulnak-e a helyi vállalatok belőle? A tanulmány megvizsgálja az elképzelést magyar feldolgozóipari vállalatokon 1992–2003 között, és éles mintát talál: igen, de csak azoknál a vállalatoknál, amelyek maguk is már elég nagyok és termelékenyek ahhoz, hogy el tudják nyelni a tudást. Kisebb, kevésbé termelékeny hazai vállalatok nem profitálnak mérhetően a szivárgó hatásokból. Az exportstátusz kevésbé számít, ha a termelékenységet kiszűrik. Az eredmény támogatja az „abszorpciós kapacitás" nézetet: a puszta FDI-vonzás önmagában nem emeli a hazai gazdaságot — a hazai vállalatoknak készen kell állniuk a tanulásra.
papers.data_used: Hungarian firm-level panel 1992–2003 from APEH/NAV corporate tax returns and balance-sheet register, covering manufacturing firms with 5+ employees; ~10,000 firms annually.
papers.data_used_hu: Magyar vállalati panel 1992–2003 az APEH/NAV társasági adóbevallásokból és a mérlegadat-nyilvántartásból, feldolgozóipari vállalatokra 5+ alkalmazottal; évente ~10 000 vállalat.
papers.policy_relevance: Direct Hungarian policy implication: FDI attraction alone is insufficient to lift domestic productivity — complementary policies that build absorptive capacity (skills, R&D, finance for SMEs) are needed. Relevant for HIPA's FDI-promotion targeting (don't assume spillovers happen automatically) and for SME support design at the Ministry of Economy (build absorptive capacity in the pipeline). Based on early-transition data (1992–2003); patterns may have shifted as Hungarian firms have matured.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Közvetlen magyar szakpolitikai következmény: az FDI-vonzás önmagában nem elegendő a hazai termelékenység emeléséhez — az abszorpciós kapacitást építő kiegészítő politikák (készségek, K+F, kkv-finanszírozás) szükségesek. Releváns a HIPA FDI-ösztönző célzásához (ne feltételezzük, hogy a szivárgó hatások automatikusak) és a Gazdasági Minisztérium kkv-támogatás tervezéséhez (abszorpciós kapacitás-építés a folyamatban). Korai átmeneti adatokra épül (1992–2003); a mintázatok időközben változhattak, ahogy a magyar vállalatok érettekké váltak.

papers.title: Temporary trade and heterogeneous firms
Measuring productivity premia with many modes of internationalization
Beszállítói termékek a magyar feldolgozóiparban
papers.abstract: A nemzetközi beszállítói láncok egyre nagyobb szerepet játszanak a modern gazdaságok termelésében és exportjában is. Egy-egy vállalat, ország vagy iparág működését alapvetően meghatározza az, hogy része-e ezeknek a globális láncoknak. Ebben a tanulmányban a magyar vállalati szintű termelési és exportadatok segítségével vizsgáljuk a köztes termékek és tőkejószágok termelését, valamint exportját a magyar feldolgozóipari vállalatok esetében. Megmutatjuk, hogy az ilyen termékek a feldolgozóipari vállalatok termelésének kétharmadát, exportjának pedig háromnegyedét teszik ki. A beszállítói termékek arányát alapvetően meghatározza a vállalat iparága, ezért a megfelelő iparágakra korlátozódó gazdaságpolitika hatásosan célozhatja meg a potenciális beszállítókat. Regressziós elemzésben bemutatjuk, hogy a tudásátterjedés lehetőségével összhangban a beszállítói termékeket gyártó vállalatok - elsősorban nagyobb méretüknek és magasabb tőkeintenzitásuknak köszönhetően - termelékenyebbek, mint a fogyasztási javakat gyártó társaik. Journal of Economic Literature (JEL) kód: D22, D24, F14, L60.

papers.title: The ladder of internationalization modes: evidence from European firms
Firms and products in international trade: Evidence from Hungary
Cultural Homophily and Collaboration in Superstar Teams
papers.abstract: One may reasonably think that cultural homophily, defined as the tendency to associate with others of similar culture, affects collaboration in multinational teams in general but not in superstar teams of professionals at the top of their industry. The analysis of an exhaustive data set on the passes made by professional European football players in the top five men’s leagues reveals that on the contrary, cultural homophily is persistent, pervasive, and consequential, even in superstar multinational teams of very-high-skill individuals with clear common objectives and aligned incentives who are involved in interactive tasks that are well defined and not particularly culture intensive. This paper was accepted by Olav Sorenson, organizations. Funding: G. Békés thanks the Hungarian Academy of Sciences [Grant “Firms, Strategy and Performance” Lendület] for support. Supplemental Material: The online appendix and data files are available at https://doi.org/10.1287/mnsc.2022.01799 .
papers.summary_en: Does culture still matter when the team is elite? Cultural homophily — the pull toward collaborating with people who share your background — is usually thought of as a feature of regular workplaces, not of top professionals whose incentives are sharply aligned. This paper tests that hunch on an unusual dataset: every pass made by players in the top five European men's football leagues. Even in these ultra-high-skill teams, players pass disproportionately to teammates from a similar cultural background, and this measurably affects team performance. The effect is persistent and pervasive. Culture is not just friction at the margin — it shapes production even at the top.
papers.summary_hu: Még a csúcsteljesítményű csapatokban is számít a kultúra? A kulturális homofília — az azonos kultúrájú emberekkel való együttműködés hajlama — általában a hétköznapi munkahelyek sajátosságának tartott, nem a csúcsszakmabelieké, akiknek ösztönzői élesen összehangoltak. A tanulmány egy szokatlan adatbázison teszteli ezt: minden passzra, amit az öt legjobb európai férfi futballiga játékosai adtak. Még ezekben a szuper-magas-képzettségű csapatokban is a játékosok aránytalanul sokat passzolnak hasonló kulturális háttérből érkező csapattársaknak, és ez mérhetően befolyásolja a csapat teljesítményét. A hatás tartós és átfogó. A kultúra nem csak peremsúrlódás — a termelést a csúcson is formálja.
papers.data_used: Near-universe of passes in the five top European men's football leagues (England Premier League, Spain La Liga, Germany Bundesliga, Italy Serie A, France Ligue 1), spanning multiple seasons; player-level data on nationality, club, and performance merged with pass-by-pass event data.
papers.data_used_hu: Az öt legjobb európai férfi futballiga (Premier League, La Liga, Bundesliga, Serie A, Ligue 1) majdnem-univerzuma a passzok szintjén, több szezonon át; játékosszintű adat nemzetiségről, klubról és teljesítményről, passzonkénti eseményadatokkal összekapcsolva.
papers.policy_relevance: For Hungarian diversity and talent policy (Ministry of Culture, universities, R&D-intensive firms): cultural homophily hurts team productivity even when incentives are otherwise well-aligned. Relevant for policies that shape the cultural diversity of Hungarian research teams, multinational firms operating in Hungary, and cross-border EU collaboration grants. Setting is football, but the authors argue the mechanism generalises. Suggests diversity interventions matter even at the very top of skill distributions, not just at entry level.
papers.policy_relevance_hu: Magyar sokszínűség- és tehetségpolitikához (Kulturális Minisztérium, egyetemek, K+F-intenzív vállalatok): a kulturális homofília még akkor is csökkenti a csapat termelékenységét, amikor az ösztönzők egyébként jól összehangoltak. Releváns olyan politikákhoz, amelyek a magyar kutatócsoportok, a Magyarországon működő multinacionális vállalatok és a határokon átnyúló EU-együttműködési ösztöndíjak kulturális sokszínűségét alakítják. A környezet futball, de a szerzők szerint a mechanizmus általánosítható. A sokszínűségi beavatkozások a készségeloszlás legtetején is számítanak, nem csak belépési szinten.

papers.title: Import prices, income, and inequality
Trade costs, quality and the skill premium
papers.journal: Canadian Journal of Economics/Revue canadienne d économique
papers.abstract: Abstract We develop a monopolistic competition model with non‐homothetic factor input bundles where increasing quality requires increasing use of skilled workers. As a result more skill abundant countries export higher quality, higher priced goods. Using a multi‐country dataset, we test and confirm the findings in Schott of a positive effect of skill abundance on unit values identified with US data. We extend the core model with per unit trade costs leading to the Washington apples effect that goods shipped over larger distance are of higher quality. The combination of high‐quality goods being relatively skill intensive with the Washington apples effect implies that countries at a larger distance from their trading partners display a higher skill premium. Simulating our model, we find that a doubling of distance of a country relative to all its trading partners raises the skill premium in a country by about 1.6%.

papers.title: Information Revelation, Liquidity Shocks, the Volatility and the Level of Bond Spreads
papers.abstract: Liquidity is often cited as an important component of asset prices, particularly for sovereign bonds. Relative to canonical explanations (asymmetric information, thin markets), this paper presents a different source of liquidity risk: in a Diamond–Dybvig type model, where agents face a liquidity shock (becoming more risk‐averse early consumers), the speed of public information revelation about default risk influences bond spreads. Under reasonable parameter values, accelerated information revelation may increase spreads by 50%. Depending on parameters, revealing information may increase or decrease not only the welfare of the issuer (the issue price), but also the expected utility of investors.

papers.title: Evaluating the effectiveness of the new EU bank regulatory framework: A farewell to bail-out?
papers.abstract: In response to the economic and financial crisis, the EU has adopted a new regulatory framework of the banking sector. Its central elements consist of new capital requirements, the single rulebook, and rules for bank recovery and resolution. These legislations have been adopted to reduce the call for government bail-out of distressed banks in future crises. The present study performs a detailed quantitative assessment of the reduction in public finance costs brought about by the introduction of these rules. We use a microsimulation portfolio model, which implements the Basel risk assessment framework, to estimate the joint distribution of bank losses at EU level. The approach incorporates the complete safety-net set up in EU legislation to absorb these losses, explicitly modelling enhanced Basel III capital rules, the bail-in tool and the resolution funds. Using a near-full sample of commercial, cooperative and savings banks in the EU, we quantify the cumulative effects of this safety-net and the contribution of each individual tool to the total effect. Considering a crisis of a similar magnitude as the recent one, our results show that potential costs for public finances decrease from roughly 3.7% of EU GDP (before the introduction of any new tool) to 1.4% with bail-in, and finally to 0.5% when all the elements we model are in place. This latter amount is very close to our estimate of leftover resolution funds and the size of the Deposit Guarantee Scheme. This exercise extends the quantitative analyses performed by the European Commission in its Economic Review of the Financial Regulation Agenda by developing additional scenarios, crucial robustness checks, simulations for different annual data vintages, and by implementing some methodological improvements.

papers.title: EVIDENCE FOR RELATIONAL CONTRACTS IN SOVEREIGN BANK LENDING
papers.journal: Journal of the European Economic Association
papers.abstract: This paper presents direct evidence for relational contracts in sovereign bank lending. Unlike the existing empirical literature, its instrumental variables method allows for distinguishing a direct influence of past repayment problems on current spreads (a “punishment” effect in prices) from an indirect effect through higher expected future default probabilities (“loss of reputation”). Such a punishment provides positive surplus to lenders after a default and decreases the borrower's present discounted value of the net benefits of future borrowing, which create dynamic incentives. Using data on bank loans to developing countries for 1973–1981 and constructing continuous variables for credit history, we find evidence that most of the influence of past repayment problems is through the direct, punishment channel.

papers.title: Building a policy relevant resilience measure: beyond the economic perspective
papers.abstract: This chapter operationalizes regional resilience using a new approach which goes beyond standard economic dimensions. A society is defined resilient if it retains the ability to deliver well-being in a sustainable way even in the case of shocks and persistent structural changes. As the ultimate objective is the maintenance of overall societal well-being, our approach does not stop at assessing only a set of selected (economic) sectors of well-being production. It aims towards a broad structure of the system which keeps track of social inclusion, social capital and quality of life in general. We illustrate our approach by concentrating on the financial and economic crisis (2008_2012) and analyse the dynamic response of EU regions. We implement a three-step methodology to (i) select a large list of economic and non-economic indicators (‘system variables’) that span the entire process of societal well-being production, (ii) compute resilience indicators as the joint dynamic response of regions to the crisis and finally (iii) look at characteristics as those pre-determined systemic features that differentiate resilient regions from non-resilient ones. Results show that resilience is highly dependent on the time horizon: there is a clear distinction between the reaction in the immediate aftermath of the crisis and in the longer run. Moreover, they confirm the importance of expanding the measurement strategy to a broader list of variables and show a substantial heterogeneity in resilience across the regions in the European Union. Finally, they highlight country and regional features (characteristics), such as private sector credit flows and the gender employment gap, as significant predictors of resilient behaviour.

papers.title: Finance and economic growth: Financing structure and non-linear impact
papers.abstract: Focusing on groups of high-income countries (OECD, EU, and EMU), this study shows that the finding of a non-linear, hump-shaped impact of financing on economic growth is robust to controlling for financing composition in terms of the sources (bank credit, debt securities, stock market) and the recipients of finances (households, non-financial and financial corporations), or both. In particular, we obtain the following results. First, the non-linear impact of total bank credit is more pronounced than that of either household credit alone, or the sum of bank credit, debt securities, and stock market financing. Second, credit to non-financial corporations tends to have a positive, while credit to households a negative impact on growth, even after allowing for non-linearities. Third, debt-securities and stock market-based financing have a different impact on growth. Finally, the estimated turning point of the non-linear relationship is close to that found by Cournède and Denk (2015) for the OECD countries, and lower than that established by Arcand, Berkes, and Panizza (2015) for a broad set of countries.

papers.title: Analysis of changes in the tax and transfer system with a behavioural microsimulation model
papers.abstract: In this study, using a new microsimulation model, we estimate the long-term fiscal and labour market effects of the changes to the tax and transfer system which were passed into law in 2010 and which are currently planned. According to our results, if all of the currently planned measures are fully implemented, the level of GDP will be increased by over 5% over the long run, while employment will increase by about 1.5% (approx. 60, 000 individuals). The estimated increase in employment is due exclusively to planned cuts in transfers. While changes to personal income tax may improve the incentives of high-income earners and thus have a stimulating effect on the economy, their effect on employment is negative due to the phasing out of the wage tax credit. These projections may change significantly if market perception of Hungary’s economic risk deteriorates. In such a case, an increase in the required return on capital may completely offset the stimulating effect of tax and transfer changes. The measures analysed also produce a substantial distributional effect. Changes to welfare benefits, as well as tax modifications, impose a burden primarily on low income households, while households with higher income generally benefit from the changes. Overall, income concentration rises from a level similar to that of Denmark or Austria to the level of Germany or Estonia, approximating the EU average.

papers.title: Assessing the economic and social impact of tax and benefit reforms: A general-equilibrium microsimulation approach applied to Hungary
